---
title: "Genotype QC pre-imputation"
---

### Overview

The pre-imputation pipeline prepares raw micro-array genotyping data for the TOPMed Imputation Server. The workflow encompasses file format conversion, sample-level QC (sex-check, heterozygosity, and duplicates), and SNP-level QC (MAF and strand ambiguity), culminating in a chromosome-split VCF set aligned to hg38.

***

### Workflow Architecture

This pipeline utilizes a mix of PLINK 1.9/2.0, BCFTools, and the specialized GenotypeQCtoHRC R suite.

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    classDef snazzy fill:#f1f1f1,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px,color:#000;

    A[Raw PED/MAP] --> B(convert_raw)
    B --> C(add_sex_to_fam)
    C --> D(rm_dup_sample)
    D --> E(check_het)
    D --> F{GenotypeQCtoHRC}
    G[1000G PGEN] --> F
    F --> H(cat_genotypes)
    H --> I(rm_maf_ambig)
    I --> J(rm_rare_snps)
    J --> K(split_chrs)
    K --> L[Final HG38 VCFs]
    K --> M(gather_stats)
    M --> N[QC Report]
    
    class A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H,I,J,K,L,M,N snazzy
    style L fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
    style N fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
```

```{mermaid}
graph LR
    classDef snazzy fill:#f1f1f1,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px,color:#000;

    A[Raw PED/MAP] --> B(convert_raw)
    B --> C(add_sex_to_fam)
    C --> D(rm_dup_sample)
    D --> E(check_het)
    D --> F{GenotypeQCtoHRC}
    G[1000G PGEN] --> F
    F --> H(cat_genotypes)
    H --> I(rm_maf_ambig)
    I --> J(rm_rare_snps)
    J --> K(split_chrs)
    K --> L[Final HG38 VCFs]
    K --> M(gather_stats)
    M --> N[QC Report]
    
    class A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H,I,J,K,L,M,N snazzy
    style L fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
    style N fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
```

::: {style="width: 70%; margin: auto;"}
```{mermaid}
graph TD
    classDef snazzy fill:#f1f1f1,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px,color:#000;

    A[Raw PED/MAP] --> B(convert_raw)
    B --> C(add_sex_to_fam)
    C --> D(rm_dup_sample)
    D --> E(check_het)
    D --> F{GenotypeQCtoHRC}
    G[1000G PGEN] --> F
    F --> H(cat_genotypes)
    H --> I(rm_maf_ambig)
    I --> J(rm_rare_snps)
    J --> K(split_chrs)
    K --> L[Final HG38 VCFs]
    K --> M(gather_stats)
    M --> N[QC Report]
    
    class A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H,I,J,K,L,M,N snazzy
    style L fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
    style N fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
:::


::: {.callout-note}

We primarily used the [GenotypeQCtoHRC](https://github.com/CardiffMRCPathfinder/GenotypeQCtoHRC) software suite 
for the bulk of the pre-imputation genotype QC. Clone the package into `../results/03GENOTYPES-PRE/GenotypeQCtoHRC/`.
As the package uses `R targets` for job scheduling, all ouputs are generated relative to the repos `HOME` directory. 
So you need to `cd` into the local repo first. The shell script in the `genotype_qc2hrc` rule handles this. Some 
additional references and packages are also required - see the GenotypeQCtoHRC documentation for details.

:::

***

### Resources

Most rules require minimal resource. Except:

| Rule | Threads | RAM (GB) | Walltime |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| `genotype_qc2hrc` | 10 | 100 | 5h | 

***

### Technical Requirements

| Category | Requirement |
| :--- | :---: | 
| Primary tools | PLINK (v1.9 & v2.0), BCFtools (v1.16), GenotypeQCtoHRC | 
| Containers | genotype-qc2hrc, R_eqtl (Singularity | 

***
***
